title: Is Chewy's Growing Vet Care Network Its Most Powerful Growth Lever?
text: Chewy's expansion into pet health services via the Chewy Vet Care (CVC) network represents a strategic move into high-value customer acquisition, driving significant ecosystem benefits. Health-related services, including Vet Care, remain structurally higher-margin than the core retail business, making them a key lever for long-term earnings growth. Gross margin expanded to 29.4% in fourth-quarter fiscal 2025, up 90 basis points year-over-year, supported by the growing mix of health and wellness offerings. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for CHWY's fiscal 2026 earnings implies year-over-year growth of 27.6%, whereas fiscal 2027 indicates an uptick of 23%. The company opened 10 Vet Care practices in fiscal 2025, reaching the high end of its planned range and bringing total footprint to 18 locations across five states. Management highlighted that Vet Care customers generate the highest and fastest Net Sales Per Active Customer (NSPAC) curves. CVC customers rapidly migrate toward high-value verticals such as premium consumables, supplemental health items, and pharmacy offerings, directly feeding Autoship-driven economics and supporting higher gross profit per customer.
